title: blemeesd.opened
description: Reply to blemeesd.open on success. `last_seq` is the highest seq the session has produced at this moment — clients can compare to their `last_seen_seq` to decide if a replay is in flight. `native_session_id` is the backend's own identifier (CC `--session-id`; Codex `threadId`).

native_session_id.description: Backend-side identifier — present *only when it differs from* `session_id`. Absence is the wire-level signal that the daemon's session_id is also the backend's id and clients can use it directly. For Claude this field is always omitted (CC's `--session-id` accepts the daemon's value verbatim). For Codex this is the `threadId`; omitted on a fresh open (unknown until the first `session_configured` event), present on resume and after the first turn.
